objective: Require tenant/scope-aware config lookup before update/delete by ID so known config IDs cannot cross tenant or global boundaries.
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated user changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
validationRun: .\mvnw.cmd -pl services/foundation-service -am test passed with 101 foundation-service tests and upstream shared-module tests.
git diff --check for touched config service/repository/test files passed with CRLF warnings only.
outcome: Config update/delete by ID now resolve mutable configs through current TenantContext tenant plus non-deleted status, and reject workspace/environment-scoped configs when current context is missing or mismatched.
residualRisks: Tenant lifecycle arbitrary-ID/list policy remains open pending a platform-admin versus self-tenant decision.
Config version history and global config administration still need explicit privileged/scope-aware workflows.
Production release remains blocked by target-environment evidence and strict release gates.
nextAction: Record completion in shared backlog/active-work memory when the current frontend metadata lease releases, then continue with differentiation exact workspace matching.
